query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "where do deep-research agents go wrong? span-level error localization " computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on computer vision (image enhancement) and deep internal learning for visual data, which is an entirely different domain from the user's question about error localization in deep-research LLM agents (natural language processing). This falls under the rejection rule for "off-domain entirely" and "distinct construct sharing only homonym keywords" (e.g., "deep" and "learning" are common terms, but the mechanisms and variables are unrelated).
